steps.title: Use the timeline
steps.content: The passage states that no source before 1155 mentions the Round Table. Annales Cambriae was composed around 970, so under the passage's own rule it could not have supplied information about the Round Table. It could still be a source for other Arthurian details, but not this one.

steps.title: Avoid unsupported inferences
steps.content: Choice B compares overall content similarity, which the passage never evaluates. Choice C contradicts the claim that no pre-1155 source had Round Table material. Choice D makes an unsupported claim about historical accuracy. The only necessary inference is that Malory could not have used Annales Cambriae for Round Table information. The answer is A.
